title: rubus × ciuriensis
intro: Rubus × ciuriensis, also known as the raspberry-leaved bramble, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between Rubus idaeus (red raspberry) and Rubus fruticosus (blackberry or bramble). The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus × ciuriensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties against certain bacteria and fungi. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
